seoTitle: How to Edit the Zendesk Knowledge Base Content
metaDescription: Learn how to edit Zendesk knowledge base articles, update text and media, optimize search tags, and republish changes to your live Help Center.
intro: Outdated troubleshooting steps or dead links in a live article can create more support tickets than they prevent. This guide covers finding, updating, and republishing existing Zendesk Guide articles so your knowledge base stays accurate.

steps.heading: Find the article to edit
steps.body: From the product matrix icon, select Knowledge, then click the Articles list.

steps.heading: Locate the specific document
steps.body: Use search or filters to isolate the published help document you want to update.

steps.heading: Open the editor
steps.body: Click Edit Article to open the WYSIWYG composer canvas.

steps.heading: Update content and media
steps.body: Update outdated text, insert fresh images, or correct broken web links throughout the article.

steps.heading: Refine search tags
steps.body: Open the right-hand control bar to revise meta tags and improve search placement for the article.

steps.heading: Preview and publish
steps.body: Check the viewport preview tool for desktop and mobile formats, then click Publish to commit your revisions live.

faq.question: Will editing an article break its existing URL?
faq.answer: No, category and section changes preserve cross-referenced structural paths without breaking active URL strings.

faq.question: Can I move an article to a different category after publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reassign edited articles to a different category folder or brand section.

faq.question: Does Zendesk keep a history of article edits?
faq.answer: Yes, the editing panel tracks historical revisions and draft states separately from the live published version.

faq.question: How do I check that my edits look right on mobile?
faq.answer: Use the interactive viewport preview tool to confirm desktop and mobile formats before publishing.
